channel.title: YouFibreGuide Blog
channel.description: Notes, updates, and tips from two-plus years of running YouFibre as the backbone of a home office and busy household.

item.title: Building an OPNsense Router on an HP ProDesk 400 G5 SFF for YouFibre
item.description: A full walkthrough: fitting an Intel X550-T2 10GbE PCIe NIC into the HP ProDesk 400 G5 SFF chassis, creating a bootable OPNsense USB installer, installing OPNsense, and doing the initial configuration for a YouFibre FTTP connection.

item.title: Stuck at 1 Gbps after upgrading to You2000? Here's why
item.description: Upgraded from You1000 to You2000 but speed tests are capping at around 940 Mbps? In the vast majority of cases the bottleneck is on the user side. A checklist covering the most common causes: PC NIC capability, cables, router hardware, QoS settings, and the ONT.

item.title: Setting up OPNsense with YouFibre FTTP
item.description: YouFibre delivers on standard DHCP, so plugging in a custom router is straightforward — but there are a few things worth knowing about MTU, the ONT handoff, and getting your WAN interface configured cleanly.

item.title: HTTPS for Self-Hosted Services: ACME, Let's Encrypt and Cloudflare DNS on OPNsense
item.description: Part 3 of 3. Use OPNsense's ACME client with a Cloudflare DNS challenge to get free, auto-renewing Let's Encrypt certificates for self-hosted services, then wire them into HAProxy. No port 80 exposure required.

item.title: HAProxy on OPNsense — Configuring Your Reverse Proxy
item.description: Part 2 of 3. Install the HAProxy plugin on OPNsense and configure frontends, backends, and ACL rules to route multiple self-hosted services by hostname on a single public IP.

item.title: What is a Reverse Proxy? Self-Hosting Explained
item.description: Part 1 of 3. A plain-English explanation of what a reverse proxy is and why you need one to host multiple services on a single IP. Covers the concepts behind HAProxy, HTTPS, and DNS challenges before the hands-on setup in Parts 2 and 3.

item.title: Two years with YouFibre: what's changed, what hasn't
item.description: A quick update after hitting the two-year mark. Pricing has moved slightly, a couple of short outages, and a new package tier. Here's whether I'd still make the same choice today.

item.title: TP-Link EAP653 Omada Wi-Fi 6 — single SSID and seamless roaming
item.description: Running multiple ceiling-mounted TP-Link EAP653 access points on a wired backhaul means every room has full-strength Wi-Fi 6 coverage. How to configure the Omada controller so all your EAPs share a single SSID and devices hand off between them seamlessly — covering fast roaming (802.11r), band steering, and minimum RSSI thresholds.

item.title: Adding a static IP to your YouFibre connection
item.description: If you're self-hosting services or running a VPN endpoint from home, a static IP makes life much simpler. YouFibre offer it as an add-on. Here's what it costs, how to request it, and how to configure it on OPNsense.
